$comment: Human-curated feed of notable additions to Gracchus. Append newest-first. Types: tranche | project | disclosure | coverage | dataset | feature. `view` is a Dashboard view id (matches VIEW_SLUGS keys); omit to render without a CTA.

entries.title: Catalogue expansion — 4 more connection stories
entries.summary: Added: JCB / Bamford family donor-contractor cluster (£19.15m donations, 70 records, 2003–2025; British Army backhoe contracts; £26m BEIS hydrogen grant); Boris Johnson post-office appointments (Daily Mail breach of ACOBA Rules + Ministerial Code, GB News approved with conditions); Sir Iain Duncan Smith Centre for Social Justice dual role (CSJ founder; Universal Credit policy lineage; Register of Members' Financial Interests); Sir John Whittingdale post-DCMS media-sector portfolio (SWNS, ALCS, FIP, plus ACOBA correspondence on the Alphasights / Betting and Gaming Council £1,000-an-hour role).

entries.title: BuyerDetail — ministerial meeting counts
entries.summary: Open a tracked department's drawer (Cabinet Office, MoD, DHSC, Home Office, BEIS, HMT etc.) and the published ministerial-meeting count for 2012–2025 now sits as a small 'Ministerial access' section. Sourced from Office of the Registrar of Consultant Lobbyists data already on the site's Lobbying view. Honest about the scope: consultant lobbying only — in-house lobbying and informal access remain outside the public record.

entries.title: PersonDetail — current-parliament declared interests for matched MPs
entries.summary: Open Sunak's PersonDetail drawer and you'll see his £3.3m current-parliament outside income alongside the historical Infosys connection. Cox shows zero current-parliament earnings (the BVI work ended) but the section makes the negative explicit — 'no outside earnings declared in current parliament' is an editorial fact too. Sourced from the Register of Members' Financial Interests via the existing 650-record MP dataset already used on the Transparency → MP Pay view. Matches by name normalisation with last-name + first-initial fallback for honorific variants ('Sir Geoffrey Cox KC' → 'Geoffrey Cox'). Peers and non-MP subjects (Mone, Hammond, Crothers, Bourne etc.) skip the section silently — they're not in the Commons register.

entries.title: Money Map — four saved 'quick view' presets
entries.summary: One click snaps the canvas to a curated cross-section: Donor-supplier overlap (firms holding contracts that funded the party in power), Defence cluster (MoD lens with people + lobbyists), Health spend cronyism (DHSC lens with donor overlaps), or Lobbyist access (consultant lobbyists and their tracked-supplier clients). Each preset sets layers, tier, minimum £, view mode and lens subject in a single move. Active preset highlighted in amber when current state matches. Sits in a 'Quick views' row above the filter chips on desktop and at the top of the mobile Filters sheet.

entries.title: Cross-link — Stories ↔ Projects, both directions
entries.summary: Open a project dossier (HS2, Universal Credit IT, Crossrail etc.) and the connection records about its contractors now surface alongside the existing supplier breakdown — 'Sir Robert McAlpine donated to Conservative party' lands on HS2's page automatically. Reverse direction too: open a Story card and a 'Projects involving [supplier]' section appears in the drawer, listing tracked Gracchus projects where the supplier is a contractor with a tap-through to each project's dossier. The catalogue stops being island-of-stories and starts behaving like a navigable graph.

entries.title: Money Map — base-layer default + Lens works for every node kind
entries.summary: First-time visitors now see the base layer (suppliers, buyers, projects, £-flow edges) by default rather than all 700+ nodes at once. People, parties, donors, lobbyists and adjacent firms are one click away — the Layers panel gains a Base-only / Show-all preset toggle. Supplier-overlap rings stay visible whatever the donor layer state — they're metadata on the supplier itself, not a separate entity. Lens mode now properly handles people, parties, donors and lobbyists as subject — click any of those bubbles or arrive via cmd-K and the canvas correctly pivots to the ego network.

entries.title: Money Map typography — brought into line with the FT/Bloomberg site scale
entries.summary: MoneyMapStyles had hard-coded font sizes from before the site-wide typography pass (6836066). Body prose, drawer text, story-card content, panel descriptions are now sized at 15–17px to match the rest of the site — not the smaller 11–14px Money Map was using since it was first built. Editorial smallness preserved where it earns its place (eyebrows, kickers, chips, citation glyphs, canvas bubble labels). Reader-prose bumped; UI chrome unchanged.

entries.title: Companies House numbers on tracked suppliers — tighter overlap matcher
entries.summary: 74 of the 576 tracked Gracchus suppliers now carry verified Companies House registration numbers, written to a sidecar at src/data/supplier-companies-house.json by a resumable precompute script (scripts/match-supplier-ch-numbers.py). Numbers were seeded in two passes from data already in the repo: Electoral Commission donor records (4 suppliers, primary source on conflict) and project-contractors.json (70 suppliers); a third optional pass calls the public CH search API once a key is configured. The donor-supplier overlap matcher in src/lib/donor-aggregation.js now runs CH-number match first (definitive) and falls back to name match second — catches subsidiaries, renamed companies, and parent groups the name-only matcher missed. Surfaces 2 new PwC donation streams that the name matcher was splitting away from supplier-pwc. A sanity guard rejects spurious CH matches where donor and supplier share no name overlap (e.g. an EC record with a stray CH number that happened to belong to an unrelated company). Closes the 'planned improvement' loop flagged in Methodology.

entries.title: Money Map — Layers panel for dial-to-taste reading
entries.summary: New floating panel on the Money Map canvas (and a Layers section in the mobile Filters sheet) lets readers toggle visibility of each node kind — suppliers, buyers, projects, people, parties, donors, lobbyists, adjacent firms, and the various edge classes. Counts shown per layer. Hiding a node kind hides edges incident to it. Replaces the standalone show-donors and show-lobbyists toggles. Lets the canvas dial from 'everything at once' to 'just the donor-supplier overlap pattern' without changing data sources.

entries.title: Money Map v2 Phase 4 — lobbyists on the canvas
entries.summary: Adds 25 registered consultant lobbyist firms to the Money Map canvas as violet rings, derived from the Office of the Registrar of Consultant Lobbyists. Each lobbyist is wired to its declared clients where those clients match a tracked Gracchus supplier (Arup, AECOM, Cook Defence, BAE-related, BT, DXC, Heathrow, Bechtel, Balfour Beatty and others). The lobbyist drawer lists tracked-supplier clients with confidence badges plus the wider declared-client roster. The register only covers consultant lobbying — in-house lobbyists and informal access remain outside the public record. New shared util src/lib/lobbyist-aggregation.js carries the tightened matcher (token-overlap with confidence tiers; rejects 'Capital City College Group → Capita plc' style false positives that the donor matcher allowed).

entries.title: Methodology updated for the connections layer
entries.summary: Methodology page now documents the Money Map v2 work: what a connection record is, the categories tracked (post-office appointment, dual role, donor-and-contractor, family ties, VIP lane referral, paid consultancy during office, donor-supplier overlap), source rigour standards, the firm-subject convention, and the matcher's known limits. Cross-linked inline to /standards and /corrections.

entries.title: Cmd-K finds people, firms, parties, donors
entries.summary: The cmd-K palette now searches across the Money Map v2 layer too. Type 'paterson', 'deloitte', 'conservative' or 'unite' and the right entity surfaces; selecting a result jumps you to the Money Map with the lens centred on it. Discovery is no longer canvas-only — every connection in the catalogue is reachable from anywhere on the site.

entries.title: Connections in Focus — stories on the homepage
entries.summary: Three featured connection stories now sit above the fold on the homepage, picked weekly by ISO-week-of-year: one regulator-finding case, one live-proceedings case, one pattern case (cross-party hedger / donor-contractor). Drags first-time visitors into the Money Map v2 work without forcing them to navigate. Same data, new front door.

entries.title: Six firm-subject Stories — the donor-contractor pattern, named
entries.summary: Six new Stories cards frame the Money Map v2 donor-supplier overlaps editorially: Deloitte (cross-party hedger £427k to both Conservative and Labour), Cook Defence Systems (Conservative £1.27m), Sir Robert McAlpine, Canary Wharf, Heathrow Airport Holdings (Reform UK — unusual), and Imagination Industries. Schema extended with kind:'firm' so the Stories surface can render company subjects alongside individual political figures.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — donor-supplier overlap surfacing live
entries.summary: Phase 3 now processes the full 6,819-record donations dataset (was: top-100 aggregate, which surfaced zero overlaps). Nine real overlaps now appear on the canvas — Deloitte LLP, Sir Robert McAlpine, William Cook Defence Systems, Canary Wharf Group, Heathrow Airport Holdings, Imagination Industries. Each is a tracked supplier whose firm also donated to a UK political party; the supplier bubble carries a coloured ring in the dominant funded party's colour. The supplier drawer now has a 'Political giving' section with per-party totals and donation date range. The Gracchus thesis — firms that hold government contracts also funding the party that gives them out — is finally legible at a glance.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — donors on the canvas + donor-supplier overlap
entries.summary: The top 25 political donors by aggregate £ (Electoral Commission record) now appear on the Money Map canvas, wired by donor→party edges in party colours. Where a tracked supplier is also on that donor list — Randox is the textbook case — the supplier bubble gains a coloured ring in the party's colour, signalling 'this firm both held government contracts and donated to the party in power'. The donor profile drawer cross-references contract and donation history side-by-side. Phase 3 of Money Map v2 — the full donor→party→minister→contract money loop is now visible.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — parties on the canvas
entries.summary: Adds political parties as a new node kind. Each person with a party affiliation now sits next to their party on the canvas, coloured in standard party colours. Click a party to see who's connected to it on this canvas, the regulator findings against its people, and the live proceedings still in play. Phase 3 will add donor-to-party edges from the Electoral Commission record.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — people on the canvas
entries.summary: The Money Map canvas now includes 19 people as a new node kind, wired from the connection records. Each person is linked to their counterparty (supplier or buyer) and the departments they served in. Click a person bubble to open a profile drawer with their roles, their connections, and the underlying source documents. Phase 2 will add political parties and donations as further node kinds.

entries.title: Connection catalogue expanded — 12 more stories
entries.summary: The Money Map Stories tab now spans Osborne (BlackRock), Clegg (Meta), Javid (JP Morgan), Hammond, Patel, May, Harding/Penrose (NHS Test & Trace), Zahawi (Balshore), Meller (PPE VIP lane), Uniserve, and Benton (lobbying suspension). Mix of regulator findings, ACOBA-approved appointments, and public-record entries. Sourced to gov.uk, parliament.uk, court records, and investigative journalism.

entries.title: Four more connection stories — Cameron, Crothers, Bourne, Cox
entries.summary: Second-wave individual-connection records from the revolving-door research brief. Cameron’s Greensill lobbying, Crothers’s dual civil-service / Greensill role, Alex Bourne’s Hancock-linked PPE contract, and Geoffrey Cox’s outside legal earnings. Sourced to Parliamentary records, Treasury Committee / PACAC findings, and investigative reporting where applicable.

entries.title: Money Map polish pass — 13 upgrades before ship
entries.summary: Shareable URLs (view/lens/tier/min/q), sticky filter bar, export-current-view-as-PNG, dashed strokes on undisclosed-£ bubbles, HHI/dependence qualitative bands (US DoJ guidelines), hover tooltips on every bubble, drawer focus trap + ARIA, collapsible mobile rails, repeat-offenders → supplier profile cross-link, weekly-rotating story cards, empty-canvas overlay with reset CTAs, discoverable Min £ cycler, and a shared useDrawerFocus hook.

entries.title: Drawer accessibility — focus trap, Esc close, empty states
entries.summary: Project, Supplier, and Buyer dossier drawers now trap focus, return it to the thing you opened them from, and close on Escape. Unresolved links render a terse 'unavailable' panel instead of crashing. Skip-to-content link wired for keyboard users.

entries.title: Story of the Week rotates weekly
entries.summary: The homepage lead cycles through the six most-overrun projects with disclosed primes, picking by ISO week-of-year so every reader sees the same pick in a given week. Current week's story is data-picked, not hand-curated.

entries.title: Citation chips in Money Map drawer + print styles
entries.summary: Money Map node drawer now exposes copy-citation chips on total-£, HHI, buyer/supplier counts, and top-edge figures. Dossiers print clean on light paper — header chrome hidden, drawer flows the full page, links append their URL.

entries.title: Citation chip on every headline figure
entries.summary: Hover any figure on the home page, in a project dossier, or in a supplier profile — a small © appears, click to copy a terse citation (finding, source file, access date). The whole tracker is now quotable without re-keying.

entries.title: Story of the Week — Universal Credit IT, 618% over
entries.summary: The home page now leads with a data-picked story: the tracked UK megaproject with the worst overrun % and a disclosed prime above £100m. Current pick: Universal Credit IT (£2.2bn → £15.8bn) — primes IBM, Accenture, DXC.

entries.title: Methodology page
entries.summary: An editorial one-pager explaining how Gracchus sources, grades, and reasons about the data — plus what we don’t claim.

entries.title: Tranche 11 — OCDS re-match
entries.summary: Re-ran the OCDS matcher against the grown cache. Fresh contract notices from Contracts Finder and Find a Tender folded into existing project dossiers.

entries.title: Supplier £ disclosure at 25.5%
entries.summary: Across all tracker projects, 236 of 924 named supplier positions now have a public £ figure attached — up from 21% pre-tranche-8.

entries.title: Supplier Rankings
entries.summary: League tables of repeat offenders, single-buyer dependencies, and buyer concentration. Every row traces back to the contracts behind it.

entries.title: Coverage Transparency page
entries.summary: Per-project disclosure broken down by source tier. Readers can see exactly where the remaining gaps are and how we plan to close them.

entries.title: Tranche 10 — description-gated dept-CSV match
entries.summary: Dept-CSV matcher now gated on shared description tokens to avoid supplier-name collisions. Unlocked £1.30bn of previously-dropped candidates.

entries.title: Department spend CSVs — MoJ, DHSC, Cabinet Office
entries.summary: Added monthly transparency CSVs for six more central departments. Backfilled to January 2022 where the publisher’s archive allows.

entries.title: Money Map — Lens mode
entries.summary: Click any entity to pivot the graph into an ego-network of its 1–2 hop neighbourhood. Makes cross-department concentration visible in one glance.
